rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/7/2023. Please supplement following information two weeks before the next hearing: 1. Notice of Hearing must be filed. 2. Explanation why Schedule A, assets on hand at beginning does not mirror the descriptions on Schedule E, property on hand at close of account period as of 10/31/2018. (as to First Republic 3447 and 3454 in comparison to 3447 and 3454). 3. Clarification why schedules report split between estate and Alice. If it is for petitioner's record keeping purpose only as the court not affirming such splits at this time. 4. Clarification of some disbursements if they should not be considered as preliminary distribution. Disbursements related to the property held by the estate only appears appropriate disbursement. Otherwise, preliminary distributions will need court approval.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WING H. LOUIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/14/2023. Please supplement the following information two weeks prior to the hearing. 1. Notice of Hearing must be given all persons who are entitled to. Probate Code 11000. 2. Petition to state the following information: (A)who the trustee of the Wing H. Louie and Mamie Louie Revocable Trust is as the sole beneficiary under the terms of the decedent's will admitted to probate. (B) who the trust beneficiaries are. If the petitioner is the sole trustee of the Trust, all trust beneficiaries must be noticed. Probate Code 11000. 3. Executor shall not make any distribution without the court's approval. Probate Code 11641.; however, Petition alleges that the estate property has been distributed to the surviving spouse. Please provide under which authority that the court can allow the action. 4. Also, petitioner appears to have distributed the asset without the court order to the surviving spouse, not to the beneficiary (the trustee of Wing H. Louie and Mamie Louie Revocable Trust) under the terms of the will. Petitioner must explain further. 5. Need allegation if Petitioner noticed to the Franchise Tax Board pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c)(1). (4) Paragraph #15 alleges that Ms. Mamie Louie waives accounting; however, no signed waiver of account on file. 6. Prayer must describe the assets subject to distribution in detail. California Rules of Court, Rule 7.651. 7. Prayer also must set forth the distribute. "to the persons entitled to it, as set forth in the petition" appears insufficient. 8. Need allegation re character of property. California Rules of Court, Rule 7.652. 9. Prayers of Petition and of Proposed Order must mirror each other. Please resubmit your proposed order updating all above information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BEVERLY WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) All parties must serve Notice of Petition to Administer Estate to ALL alleged heirs at law as well as ALL persons named in the purported will. PC 8110. (2) Contestants to report the court if PC 8250(a) has been met. (3) Counsel for competing petitioners (Mr. Whitfield and Ms. Maximo) must cure the following procedural issues: (A) Need Proof of Publication; (B) Need Duties and Liabilities of each proposed administrator; (C) Need Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number from each proposed administrator; (D)Need Clarification of Bond amount. Based on the Petition #3.d, $487,000 appears appropriate; (E)Petition to supplement Carolyn West's address on Attachment #8. No address listed; and (F)Need Proposed Order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT G. PIETSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/28/23. (No earlier date available.) No proposed order has been submitted or received. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] The petitioner was one of just two witnesses to the will, under which she is the primary beneficiary. [Probate Code Section 6112(c).] Nothing appears to have been filed to rebut the presumption that the witness procured the devise by duress, menace, fraud, or undue influence. The will names Carolyn Schreiber in Rocklin, CA, as a beneficiary. Carolyn "Shriver" in Roseville, CA, is listed in item 8 and received notice without any clarification about her identity. The will provision in paragraph 3 of the FIFTH section is a little unclear, but San Francisco State University is not listed in item 8 of the petition and did not get notice. The proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/17/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
